mnemonics.title: File Stream Classes Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: IF you want to read from file: use IFstream (I For reading), OFstream for writing (O For output), Fstream for both.

mnemonics.title: File Mode Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: OPEN: Out - write, In - read, Trunc - truncate, App - append, Bin - binary, Ate - at end.

acronyms.expansion: File Stream - provides both input and output file operations
acronyms.usage: Used when you need to both read from and write to the same file

acronyms.expansion: Input File Stream - for reading files
acronyms.usage: Use when only reading data from a file

acronyms.expansion: Output File Stream - for writing to files
acronyms.usage: Use when only writing data to a file

acronyms.expansion: Input Output Stream - base for stream I/O operations
acronyms.usage: Provides constants for file opening modes like ios::in, ios::out

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a door - ios::in enters to read, ios::out exits to write, ios::app knocks at end before entering, ios::trunc demolishes before building new, ios::binary is a special delivery entrance.
Think of a tree: ios is the trunk, istream and ostream are branches, ifstream and ofstream are leaves, fstream is a special branch with both reading and writing fruits.
Like a loading dock - data collects in buffer (dock) before being transferred to file (warehouse). close() is like signing the delivery slip to send everything.

analogies.analogy: Opening a file is like opening a book - ofstream is like a notebook where you write, ifstream is like reading a book, fstream is like a diary you both read and write in.
ios::app is like adding pages to the end of a document, ios::trunc is like using a fresh page, ios::binary is like a locked safe (raw data without interpretation).
Closing a file is like leaving a library - you must check out (close) to ensure your notes (data) are saved and others can use the resource (file handle).
